derived_from: static inspection of tools/benchmark/fixture/src/ledgerkit/report.py at the pinned base -- total_balance, category_totals, and largest_category have no docstrings today

tools/benchmark/ground-truth/ledgerkit-defects.sealed.json, defect D-004 (category_totals silently skips entries on accounts with category is None) -- this task does not require fixing D-004, only documenting the current behavior accurately, so an accurate category_totals docstring must name this skip rather than describing it as covering every touched account
content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: total_balance, category_totals, and largest_category in src/ledgerkit/report.py each have a docstring (none did at the pinned base).
items.how_to_detect: Read src/ledgerkit/report.py: confirm all three functions now have a non-empty docstring.

items.description: The correctness trap: category_totals' docstring explicitly states that entries on an account with no category (category is None) are excluded/skipped from the result -- it does not claim or imply that every touched account is reflected.
items.how_to_detect: Read category_totals' implementation (the `if account.category is None: continue` line) and compare against its new docstring: the docstring must name this exclusion, not describe the function as covering every account touched by a transaction.

items.description: largest_category's docstring states what it returns for a ledger with no categorized entries (None) and describes its tie-break behavior accurately -- i.e. that ties are resolved deterministically (by category name), not silently glossed over as arbitrary or unmentioned.
items.how_to_detect: Read largest_category's implementation (max(..., key=lambda kv: (kv[1], kv[0]))) and confirm the docstring both states the empty-ledger return value and correctly characterizes the tie-break (alphabetically-last category name wins a tie) rather than omitting it or describing it as arbitrary/undefined.

items.description: total_balance's docstring states its return for an empty ledger and correctly characterizes when it is expected to be zero (every posted transaction balances individually, so the ledger-wide sum is always zero) rather than leaving that unstated or implying it can be arbitrary.
items.how_to_detect: Read total_balance's implementation and account_balances/Ledger.post's balancing invariant; confirm the docstring states the empty-ledger case and the always-zero-for-a-valid-ledger property (or explicitly and correctly describes why/when it wouldn't be).

items.description: No behavior was changed -- this is a documentation-only task; category_totals still silently skips uncategorized entries exactly as before (the docstring documents this, it does not fix it).
items.how_to_detect: Diff report.py's function bodies against the base: only docstrings were added, no logic changed.

items.description: Docstring style matches the rest of report.py (short summary line, plain prose, consistent with account_balances' and running_totals_by_category's existing docstrings) -- no invented markup convention foreign to the file.
items.how_to_detect: Compare the new docstrings' formatting/tone against the two pre-existing docstrings in the same file.

items.description: pytest tests/ and ruff check . still pass (doc-only change must not break anything).
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root; both must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
